“I will extol the Lord at all times; his praise will always be on my lips. I will glory in the Lord; let the afflicted hear and rejoice. Glorify the Lord with me; let us exalt his name together. I sought the Lord, and he answered me; he delivered me from all my fears. Those who look to him are radiant; their faces are never covered with shame. This poor man called, and the Lord heard him; he saved him out of all his troubles. The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and he delivers them. Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him. Fear the Lord, you his holy people, for those who fear him lack nothing. The lions may grow weak and hungry, but those who seek the Lord lack no good thing. Come, my children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the Lord. Whoever of you loves life and desires to see many good days, keep your tongue from evil and your lips from telling lies. Turn from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it. The eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears are attentive to their cry; but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil, to blot out their name from the earth. The righteous cry out, and the Lord hears them; he delivers them from all their troubles. The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit. The righteous person may have many troubles, but the Lord delivers him from them all; he protects all his bones, not one of them will be broken. Evil will slay the wicked; the foes of the righteous will be condemned. The Lord will rescue his servants;no one who takes refuge in him will be condemned.” Psalms 34: 1 - 22 NIV

@@sunbeamsomewhere: We are living in very difficult times— times I never imagined I would witness. In the land of freedom , the country I call home, I have seen situations that have deeply troubled my heart. I do not speak from a political position, nor from anger or division. I speak from conscience, faith, and a sincere concern for what is right. I believe that remaining silent in the face of injustice does not preserve peace; rather, it allows harmful actions to continue without reflection or correction. I do not believe that the loss of innocent life should ever be considered normal. I do not believe that any authority, acting without transparency, identification, or accountability, should operate beyond the boundaries of the law. These concerns are not political— they are moral and constitutional. I believe in the United States Constitution and the principles upon which it was established. The Constitution protects freedom of speech and peaceful expression (First Amendment). It safeguards individuals from unreasonable searches and seizures (Fourth Amendment). It affirms that no person shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of law (Fifth Amendment), and it upholds equal protection under the law (Fourteenth Amendment). These principles exist to protect both liberty and human dignity. There is a clear and necessary distinction between enforcing the law against criminals— such as thieves, traffickers, murderers, and those who harm others— and the misuse of power that results in harm to innocent people. Justice requires restraint, training, accountability, and respect for life. These values must never be confused or diminished.

@There is Hope in God:To the general public, I'm writing with a heavy heart, shattered by the news of my dear friend, an ICU RN, who was tragically shot today by ICE agents in Minnesota during a demonstration. He was selflessly trying to save a woman from arrest, and instead, he lost his life. He was a beacon of hope, compassion, and kindness – a true hero who dedicated his life to saving others. His passion, love, and hospitality inspired everyone around him. This senseless act of violence raises questions about our nation's values. Where are we headed, America? When did we become a country that kills its own people? The America I knew, the land of the free and home of the brave, seems lost. As an immigrant who sought refuge here, I'm left wondering if I've made a mistake. My heart is broken, and words escape me. I'm left with tears and a deep sorrow. Rest in peace, dear friend. Your memory will live on in our hearts. (Signed with tears and grief) Franklin Boak
